mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market triggers recession fears that override earnings expectations, causing immediate equity sell-offs. When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within three months, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous industrial production expansion and consumer price deceleration signal a supply-side recovery that boosts corporate margins without triggering inflationary rate hikes. This joint configuration produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 55% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns do not differ significantly from periods where only INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, rising policy rates increase the cost of capital and compress equity valuations. When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25 percent and continues to rise, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days are systematically negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25% and continues to rise
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns in tightening episodes average positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during the 2015-2019 hiking cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, driving positive equity returns over short horizons. Quarterly GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate generates positive forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.10 over any 5-year window, or forward returns show no directional consistency.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial output falling below its long-term trend indicates capacity constraints and demand weakness that erode corporate profitability. When the industrial production index drops below 98.0, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days turn negative.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of instances where INDPRO breaches the 98.0 threshold, or the signal exhibits no incremental predictive power over a simple moving average benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between strong employment growth and weakening housing starts signals an economy overheating in services but contracting in capital-intensive sectors. This labor-housing mismatch predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as sectoral rotation takes hold.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive predictive accuracy below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test, or forward returns do not significantly underperform when PAYEMS rises and HOUST falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer credit demand reflects strong household balance sheets and liquidity availability, supporting near-term equity valuations. When revolving consumer credit exceeds 600 billion dollars, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days are positive.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn negative or statistically insignificant when RSAFS breaches the 600 billion threshold, or the signal fails to predict outperformance during the 2008-2012 deleveraging period.
